Pricing.title: Our plans and
Pricing.subtitle: Pick the right plan for your business or personal blog. Switch at any time.
Pricing.footerNote: * All payments are processed through a manual invoice system. Once your account is created, choose a package and receive an invoice. Yearly plans include 2 extra months at no charge.

Faq.heading: Questions &
Faq.subtitle: Find answers to the most common questions about JetBlog. Search below or browse by category.
Faq.notFound: Didn't find an answer? Go to Docs →
Faq.ctaBody: Check the full documentation or reach out to support.
Faq.g1q: What is JetBlog and who is it for?
Faq.g1a: JetBlog is a platform that uses AI to write SEO articles and automatically publish them to your site. Built for bloggers, digital marketing agencies, and multi-site businesses. You just enter keywords — JetBlog handles everything else.
Faq.g2q: What do I get with the free plan?
Faq.g2a: The free plan includes: 1 site connection, 10 keyword analyses per month, and 2 article generations. When credits run out generation pauses, but your site and data are never deleted.
Faq.g3q: How does the AI write articles?
Faq.g3a: You enter a keyword → Claude (Anthropic) writes an SEO-optimised, human-sounding article → DALL-E 3 creates a cover image → it's automatically published to your site. Configure Brand Voice and the article's style and tone will fully match your site.
Faq.g4a: We use Anthropic Claude Sonnet — one of the highest-quality AI models available. With Brand Voice configured, the voice and style are fully tailored to yours. You can also review and edit every article in the TipTap editor before publishing.
Faq.g5q: How many credits does one article cost?
Faq.g5a: Each article generation costs 1 credit. The number of credits depends on your plan. When credits run out you can buy additional credits or upgrade your plan.
Faq.g6a: No. Additionally purchased credits never expire. Only the monthly plan credits (the monthly limit) don't roll over to the next month.
Faq.g7a: Yes. WordPress Application Passwords and other secret keys are stored with AES-256-GCM encryption. No password is stored in plain text. Supabase Row Level Security protects all user data.
Faq.g8a: Via Telegram: the link is in Dashboard → Settings → Support. We typically respond within 1 business day.
Faq.p1q: Which platforms does it work with?
Faq.p1a: WordPress, Ghost CMS, Webflow, and any other platform via webhook (Next.js, Nuxt, Laravel, etc.). Each site can use a different platform.
Faq.p2a: WP Admin → Users → Profile (or Your Profile) → scroll down → Application Passwords → create a new password. In JetBlog Connections enter URL, username, and application password → Test connection.
Faq.p3q: What is a WordPress Application Password?
Faq.p3a: A secure API authentication method available since WordPress 5.6. Completely separate from your main admin password — works only for API requests. Can be deleted instantly from the WP panel at any time.
Faq.p4q: How do I connect Ghost CMS?
Faq.p4a: Ghost Dashboard → Settings → Integrations → Add custom integration → name it (e.g. JetBlog) → copy the Admin API Key. In JetBlog select the Ghost platform and enter your Ghost URL and Admin API Key.
Faq.p5q: What do I need to use Webflow?
Faq.p5a: Webflow Starter plan ($14/mo) or higher is required — the free plan doesn't support the CMS API. API token: Project Settings → Integrations → API Access. Collection ID: CMS → Collections → ID in the URL.
Faq.p6q: Can I connect a custom site (Next.js, Nuxt, Laravel...)?
Faq.p6a: Yes. Webhook works with any technology. About 20 lines of code are added to your site — articles arrive automatically. Ready-made examples for Node.js, PHP and Python are at /docs/webhook.
Faq.p7a: Every request includes an HMAC-SHA256 signature (X-JetBlog-Signature header). Your site verifies the signature — tampered or fake requests are rejected. The secret key is known only to you and JetBlog.
Faq.p8q: How many platforms can I connect to one account?
Faq.p8a: Each site can use a different platform. For example: site 1 — WordPress, site 2 — Ghost, site 3 — Webhook. The number of sites is limited by your plan.
Faq.p9q: Where is the article published in Webflow?
Faq.p9a: Into the CMS Collection you specify. You enter the Collection ID in connection settings. JetBlog fills in the name (title), slug, and content fields.
Faq.p10q: What should I do if my site URL changes?
Faq.p10a: Open the site card on the Connections page → Edit → save the new URL → Test connection. Articles linked to the old URL remain unchanged.
Faq.b1q: What is the difference between credits and a plan?
Faq.b1a: A plan is a monthly limit (number of articles, sites, keywords). Credits are individual units purchased separately for extra articles beyond the plan limit. When the plan limit runs out, you can buy credits and continue.
Faq.b2q: Will credits be lost when I upgrade my plan?
Faq.b2a: No. Additionally purchased credits are preserved when you change plans. The new plan's limit is added on top of your saved credits.
Faq.b3q: What happens when the monthly limit runs out?
Faq.b3a: Article generation stops automatically. You have two options: buy additional credits (Settings → Billing) or wait for the start of the next month (limit resets).
Faq.b4q: How many users are in the Agency plan?
Faq.b4a: Currently 1 user. Multi-user (team members) functionality is in development and coming soon.
Faq.b5a: Currently: Visa and Mastercard. Cryptocurrency and bank transfer will be added in the future.
Faq.b6a: Yes — full refund within 14 days if no articles have been generated. Email support@jetblog.app.
Faq.t1a: Typically 30–90 seconds. Longer articles (3000+ words) may take up to 2 minutes. Do not close the page while generating.
Faq.t2a: Yes. DALL-E 3 creates a unique featured image matching the article topic for every article. The image is uploaded to WordPress/Ghost/Webflow and attached to the article.
Faq.t3q: Can I edit an article before publishing?
Faq.t3a: Yes. Open the article in Content Queue → edit it in the TipTap editor → click Publish. The article stays in draft until you're ready.
Faq.t4a: In site settings you set publish_days (publishing days) and publish_time (time, UTC). CRON checks daily — articles whose time has come are published automatically. Autopilot must be active.
Faq.t5q: What is Google Search Console used for?
Faq.t5a: With GSC connected, article recommendations are based on your site's real search data (keywords, clicks, positions). Optional but recommended — articles built on real data drive more traffic.
Faq.t6a: Yes. The JetBlog API works via Supabase JWT token. Full endpoint documentation is at /docs/api-endpoints. Currently only for authenticated users; external API keys are coming soon.
